title: Salesforce vs HubSpot
description: Head-to-head comparison of Salesforce's enterprise CRM power versus HubSpot's accessible inbound marketing platform.

features.name: Marketing Automation
features.tool2Detail: Native Marketing Hub with email, ads, social

features.name: Reporting & Dashboards
features.tool2Detail: Custom report builder at Enterprise tier ($3,600/mo)

features.name: AI Features (Einstein / Breeze)
features.tool1Detail: Einstein AI across sales, service, marketing

features.name: Customization (Fields, Layouts, Objects)
features.tool1Detail: Custom objects, validation rules, Apex code
features.tool2Detail: Custom properties only, no custom objects

features.name: Sales Sequences / Engagement
features.tool2Detail: Native sequences with email tracking, meeting links

features.name: Built-in Content Management
features.tool2Detail: Native CMS Hub with pages, blog, SEO

verdict: HubSpot offers a dramatically simpler setup with transparent freemium pricing and native CRM-to-marketing automation integration that lets small teams go live in hours. Salesforce provides unmatched enterprise customization with custom objects, workflows, AppExchange integrations, and Einstein AI forecasting that scales to the most complex sales processes. Small-to-medium businesses that need a CRM that works out of the box with integrated marketing tools will prefer HubSpot's all-in-one approach. Large enterprises with dedicated admin teams and complex sales workflows should choose Salesforce for its limitless customization and enterprise scalability.

faqs.question: Which is better, Salesforce or HubSpot?
faqs.answer: HubSpot is better for SMBs and mid-market companies that want a CRM that works out of the box with integrated marketing tools. Salesforce is better for large enterprises with complex sales processes, custom objects, and dedicated admin teams to manage the platform.

faqs.question: Can Salesforce and HubSpot be used together?
faqs.answer: Yes, through the HubSpot-Salesforce integration connector. Some companies use Salesforce as their primary CRM while leveraging HubSpot for marketing automation and lead generation, syncing data bidirectionally.

faqs.question: Which is more affordable, Salesforce or HubSpot?
faqs.answer: HubSpot is significantly more affordable. Its free CRM includes unlimited users and essential features. Paid plans start at $50/month per seat. Salesforce's entry-level plans start around $25/user/month but require more expensive tiers to access core features like pipeline management and reporting.

faqs.question: Which is better for small teams?
faqs.answer: HubSpot is vastly better for small teams. Its free CRM provides contact management, deal tracking, and email integration with no user limits. Salesforce's complexity and cost structure are difficult for small teams without dedicated admin support to justify.
